free_text: Quasi-free scattering of polarized protons.
Target cell consisted of three sections, each 50mm
in diameter and 1 mm thick and separated by Mylar
foils 0.025 mm thick. Centre section was filled with
water while the two outer sections contained air at
atmospheric pressure. The air in the outer section
maintained sufficient pressure on the water in the
inner section to prevent it from boiling when the
target was placed in the evacuated scattering chamber.
No reply from C.A. Miller to author proof.
Data scanned from figures in reference by CAJaD.
CAJaD + VM. Replaces data set O0064.
